home.setupDescription: Configure Justif in a few steps to manage your expense reports.
home.welcomeDescription: Sign in to access your expenses and exports.
home.sessionExpiredDescription: Your session has expired. Sign in again to continue.

controller.title: Data controller
controller.body: This application is self-hosted. The data controller is the person or organisation that operates this Justif instance, not the Justif open-source project. Please contact your administrator for any privacy-related request.

collected.title: Data collected
collected.intro: This application stores the following data:
collected.credentials: Account credentials (email address and hashed password)
collected.expenses: Expense records entered by users (amounts, dates, categories, vendor names)
collected.auditLogs: Audit log entries: action type, timestamp, affected entity, and the IP address of the request (see below)

ip.title: IP address collection
ip.body: Every sensitive action performed in the application (sign-in, expense creation or deletion, data export, user or settings changes) records the client IP address in the audit log. This is done for the following reasons:
ip.reason1: Detect unauthorised access attempts and brute-force attacks
ip.reason2: Trace administrative actions to a network origin for compliance purposes
ip.reason3: Support internal and external audit investigations
ip.basis: The legal basis for this processing is the legitimate interest of the operator in maintaining the security and integrity of the application and its data.

retention.title: Data retention
retention.body: Audit log entries are retained for as long as the administrator of this instance configures. When a user account is deleted, the user identifier in existing audit log entries is removed (pseudonymisation), but the log entries themselves are preserved to maintain audit trail integrity as required by compliance obligations.

rights.title: Your rights
rights.intro: Under the GDPR you have the following rights regarding your personal data:
rights.access: Right of access — you may request a copy of the data held about you
rights.erasure: Right to erasure — you may request deletion of your account and associated personal data
rights.erasureNote: Note: audit log entries are pseudonymised rather than fully deleted upon account erasure, as they may be required for legal or compliance purposes
rights.portability: Right to data portability — you may request your data in a structured, machine-readable format
rights.contact: To exercise any of these rights, contact the administrator of this Justif instance directly.

setup.title: Welcome to Justif
setup.subtitle: Create your admin account to get started
setup.ocrDescription: Choose how to analyze your receipts.

dashboard.singleMonth: Single month — chart requires at least 2 data points

expenses.title: Expenses
expenses.exportPermissionDenied: You don't have permission to export this user's data

exportDialog.title: Some expenses have already been exported
exportDialog.description: Some expenses in this selection have already been included in a previous expense report. Choose which ones to include again in this export:

upload.title: Import a receipt
upload.dropzone: Drop a file here or click to select
upload.ocrError: OCR analysis failed — you can fill in the fields manually

settings.title: Settings
settings.ocrPromptOverrideHelp: Extra instructions appended to the locked extraction prompt (e.g. supplier-specific quirks). Leave blank to use the default behavior.
settings.ocrPromptOverridePlaceholder: e.g. Always double-check the VAT amount printed near the total.
settings.extractReferenceNumberHelp: When enabled, OCR actively looks for a highlighted reference or ticket number (e.g. boxed, circled in red) and fills the Reference field. The field stays editable either way.
settings.requireValidationHelp: When enabled, expenses must be submitted for review and approved by an administrator before they can be exported.
